source: CT6-6 §13 (REPO-1…4) — the service-tier separation manifest + the machine-checkable separability invariant; the FINAL CT6-6 component (completes the program)

scenarios: the manifest is well-formed and a malformed one is reported invalid
acceptance_criteria: SEPARATION_MANIFEST: open-core-vs-paid plan; every service separable (REPO-3); adapter seams; paid/closed pieces (SEC-4 attestation, SMP-4 entitlement) each mapped to a real seam
validate_manifest checks shape + reports invalid (never raises) on malformed/non-dict input
services/SEPARATION_MANIFEST.md documents the plan + seam table + separate-out procedure

scenarios: a nested module-load external import is caught; a lazy one is allowed
acceptance_criteria: check_separation parses every services/**/*.py; import-clean = stdlib + in-repo only at module load
any external module-load import is a violation (inject via a seam, never hard-import)
SOUND against nesting: catches imports in try/except, if, with, for, class bodies; allows in-function (lazy) imports
the real services/ tree is reported clean
services/separation.py stdlib-only; DESIGNS + VALIDATES the boundary; the actual repo SPLIT is a future op
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1
independent adversarial review FIX-FIRST -> remediated 3 (scanner-nesting soundness, validate crash, 3.10+ guard)

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only manifest + invariant + tests).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — the component makes no network/DB call; it statically parses the in-repo services/ tree. The invariant is exercised by the pytest suite over the real tree + synthetic tmp-dir cases.

confirmed_stubs.why: REPO-3 says design-for-separation NOW, split LATER; this change encodes + validates the boundary, the physical split (creating the paid/closed repo) is a future operation — documented, not a gap

confirmed_stubs.stub: the paid/closed implementations behind the seams
confirmed_stubs.why: the SEC-4 attestation algorithm + the SMP-4 entitlement/billing live in the future closed repo and are injected via the documented seams (attestation_verifier / entitlements_for); the open core ships the stubs — by design, not a gap
